summary: Created MPI-162 — shrink LTX text-encoder: A/B GGUF Gemma Q4_K_M(7.3GB)+Q5_K_M(8.44GB) + abliterated LoRA vs the 14.5GB fp8 heretic. The real LTX speed lever (slowness is RAM/load-bound, Gemma=half the footprint). UNLOCK: official workflow uses STOCK gemma + abliterated LoRA (not baked heretic) so any GGUF gemma works; and ships fp4 itself (contradicts our fp4-hurts note). DualCLIPLoader(GGUF) inherits the ltxv type from the stock node -> ltxv very likely supported, mixed gguf+bf16 likely works. bartowski URLs in card. Builder-Pod A/B: quality (faces/prompt/uncensor) + speed/RAM incl low-RAM Pod.

Refined (user): abliterated LoRA is UNTESTABLE for us — prior tests (gore/NSFW) found no pin-downable difference; only firm finding was specialized-Gemma >= LoRA. So the LoRA is a SHIP-IT HEDGE, NOT a test gate. Keep it in (official-workflow wiring, ~0.5GB, uncensor insurance moving off heretic Gemma), do NOT block on isolating its effect. The real decider = stock-GGUF-Gemma + LoRA matches fp8-heretic quality while ~7GB lighter + faster.
